channel.title: Comments for page "Cut Up While Thinking"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Cut Up While Thinking"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just amazing. I am at a loss of words.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering some of the confused things real!Reagan said, it make you wonder if the tape isn't bleeding through into reality …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so yeah, dont give sumerian gods windows movie maker.they use the static way too much
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoa that was freaky. +1

item.title: Whoa
item.content:encoded:
I never made the connection between the hooded figure and the Abu Gharaib hooded man (Ali Shallal al-Qaisi) until now.

I looked at his Wikipedia page and he's quoted as saying:

"I'm spending sleepless nights thinking about the agony I went through,"

"I even have recurring nightmares that I'm in my cell at Abu Ghraib, cell 49 as they called it, being tortured at the hands of the people of a great nation that carries the torch of freedom and human rights."

This is a very intense image. A symbol of the hypocrisy of the American government tormenting a leader, presumably as a sort of symbolic karma for his support of the military-industrial complex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speechless. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, the SCP this goes to is one of the very few new ones that I really enjoyed.

This is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely disturbing and depressing. A great addition to one of my favorite SCPs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got the impression that the two were slowly becoming one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this, though I found it more sad than creepy, especially the whole part with Reagan being rescued by his doctors and/or Secret Service.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1981 is one of my my favorite SCPs, but was unaware of Dr. Robinson's Statement prior to this comment. So, thank you author, for this piece, and thank you Smapti for Dr. Robinson.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My understanding of this is that the entity took pieces of Reagan's mind, and created 1981 from them, and his dementia is the result of those pieces being missing. So the Reagans from 1981 are not from other universes, they're from ours, incomplete twisted pieces of the man, and when he watched the tapes, he reestablished some sort of connection with what was once a part of him and that's why he can't differentiate between what happened in reality and what happened in 1981.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty good. Based on the references hidden in it, I assume this is meant to be Reagan's thoughts as he's slipping into dementia after watching the tape in Dr. Robinson's Statement, which is a nice little easter egg as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That or he's our universes' Roger Smith, and the amnesia affects -all- Roger/Reagans, regardless of their place in space and time. Ours just fought it off for longer. The ones from the various 1981 verses? They cast in the name of God, and were guilty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see what you're going for here, it just didn't do anything for me.

EDIT: 2013 OS had shit taste. Vote reversed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw and see 1981 as an interesting, very well-written piece of surreal horror, rather than actually being scary or even that creepy. So reading what, to my understanding, amounted to an extended log from the main article did nothing for me. I began scrolling through the article trying to find something that would elevate to something worthwhile. Unfortunately, I was unable to. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew from the very beggining what it was, but that didn't stop it to hit my face with awesome creepyness. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's Fing crazy talk up in here! I feel like the world was collapsing in on itself as I read this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well then. I shoulda known who the narrator was by the author's name.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well… That was… that. I can't really think of anything of anything to compare this to. It's good. Very good. Gives me a new way of looking at 1981. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got the impression that it was a combination. Anyway, this is confusing in a very creepy way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that's fucking spooky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the most disturbing and cleverly unsettling things I've ever read on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ho. Lee. Shit.

Took me a little bit to realize who the narrator was, the moment I did I scrolled back up and upvoted.

Headcanon: The speeches in 1981 are all real speeches given by Ronald Reagan… somewhere. Watching 1981 "downloaded" into Reagan the memories from the other Reagans in other universes. He never lost his memories, he just became incapable of telling the memories from this universe from the memories in other universes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking outstanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's either the point of view of real-life Reagan, or a Reagan inside the tape. Both would be hellish existences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that you were not paid for this is a crime against humanity.

Utterly fantastic.
